token
style
pattern

DSDS Link Definition

A typed reference to an external resource or a related artifact. Used across all DSDS document types for source code, design files, documentation, packages, and artifact relationships.

Source: common/link.schema.json

link

A typed reference to an external resource or a related artifact. Links serve dual purposes: pointing to external resources (source code, design files, documentation, packages) and expressing relationships to other DSDS artifacts (alternatives, parents, children). The `type` property classifies the reference. Standard external types: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository'. Standard relationship types: 'alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related'. Custom values are permitted.

object

PropertyTypeRequiredDescription
typestringrequiredThe category of the linked resource or relationship. Standard external types: 'source', 'design', 'storybook', 'documentation', 'package', 'repository'. Standard relationship types: 'alternative', 'parent', 'child', 'related'. Custom values are permitted and SHOULD be lowercase strings matching ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$.
urlstring (uri)requiredThe URL of the linked resource. MUST be a valid absolute URI. For related DSDS artifacts, this SHOULD point to the artifact's documentation page or DSDS document.
labelstringoptionalA human-readable label describing the specific resource or relationship (e.g., 'React component source', 'Design file — variants', 'Link component (alternative)').
↑ Back to top
